tasks.name: Save 3 recent devotionals about anxiety under 10 minutes to your reading list
tasks.description: Find daily devotionals on the topic of anxiety, filter them to those published within the last 12 months and with a reading time of 10 minutes or less, then add the first 3 matching devotionals to your personal reading list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the “Devotionals” item in the main navigation menu.
Click the search bar at the top of the Devotionals page and type “anxiety”, then press Enter.
Click the “Filters” button in the sidebar and select the “Anxiety” topic tag if it is not already selected.
In the filters panel, set the maximum reading time to “10 minutes” and set the date range to “Last 12 months,” then click the “Apply Filters” button.
Scroll through the filtered devotionals list and click the title of the first devotional in the list that matches the filters to open its detail page.
On the devotional detail page, click the “Save to Reading List” or “Add to My Devotionals” button.

Use the browser back button or the site’s “Back to Devotionals” link to return to the filtered devotionals list, then repeat the open-and-save process for the next two devotionals in the list so that you have added a total of 3 anxiety-related devotionals under 10 minutes from the last 12 months to your reading list.

tasks.name: Reserve seats for a 9:00 AM Sunday service for your family
tasks.description: Find the next upcoming Sunday 9:00 AM service and submit a reservation for 3 people (2 adults and 1 child age 7), selecting a front-section seating preference.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, hover over or click “Gatherings” and select “Sunday Services” from the dropdown menu.
On the Sunday Services page, locate the schedule or list of upcoming services and find the next service labeled with a start time of “9:00 AM” on a Sunday date.
Click the “Reserve Seats” or “Register” button associated with that 9:00 AM Sunday service.
In the reservation form, set the number of adult attendees to “2” and the number of child attendees to “1,” and if requested, enter the child’s age as “7.”
In the seating preference or section dropdown, select the option labeled “Front Section” or the closest equivalent front-area seating option.
Fill in the contact fields using sample data, such as entering “Alex Parker” as the full name and “alex.parker@example.com” as the email address, and a 10-digit phone number in the phone field.
Click the “Submit Reservation” or “Confirm Seats” button to complete the reservation for 2 adults and 1 child at the 9:00 AM Sunday service with a front-section seating preference.

tasks.name: Create a 30-day Old Testament Bible reading plan starting on June 1, 2026 with daily reminders
tasks.description: Set up a custom Bible reading plan that covers only Old Testament passages, lasts 30 days, starts on June 1, 2026, assigns 4 chapters per day, and enables daily reminder notifications at 7:00 AM.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Bible Reading Plans” or “Plans” link in the main navigation.
On the Plans page, click a button labeled “Create Plan,” “New Plan,” or similar to start a custom plan.
In the plan type or content section, select the “Old Testament” option so that only Old Testament readings are included.
In the duration or length field, enter “30” and select “days” if needed, then in the chapters-per-day field or setting, enter “4.”
Click the date picker for the plan start date and select “June 1, 2026” from the calendar.
Scroll to the notifications or reminders section and toggle on daily reminders, then set the reminder time to “7:00 AM” using the time selector.

In the plan name field, type a title such as “30-Day Old Testament Plan,” then click the “Save Plan,” “Create Plan,” or equivalent button to finalize the 30-day plan starting June 1, 2026 with 4 chapters per day and 7:00 AM reminders.

tasks.name: Submit an anonymous health-related prayer request and opt into the weekly prayer newsletter
tasks.description: Use the prayer request form to submit a request categorized as Health, mark it as anonymous, and opt in to receive the weekly prayer newsletter.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Prayer” or “Prayer Requests” link in the main navigation or footer.
On the Prayer page, click the “Submit a Prayer Request” or “Share Your Request” button.
In the prayer request form, open the category dropdown and select the category labeled “Health” or the closest health-related option.
In the request text area, type a short sample request such as “Please pray for healing and strength during an upcoming medical procedure.”
Locate the privacy or anonymity option on the form and check the box or select the setting that indicates the request should be posted or shared as “Anonymous.”

Find the newsletter or updates section on the form and check the box or toggle to opt into the “Weekly Prayer Newsletter,” then click the “Submit Request” or “Send” button to send the anonymous health-related prayer request with newsletter opt-in.

tasks.name: Make a one-time $50 donation to the Missions Fund and cover the 2.5% processing fee
tasks.description: Complete a donation form to give a one-time $50 gift to the Missions Fund, choose to cover a 2.5% processing fee, pay by credit card, and mark the donation as given in honor of someone.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Give” or “Donate” option in the main navigation.
On the giving page, open the fund dropdown and select the fund labeled “Missions Fund” or the closest missions-related option.
In the amount field, type “50” to set the one-time donation amount to $50 and ensure the frequency option is set to “One-Time” instead of recurring.
Locate the option to cover processing fees and check the box or toggle labeled “Cover 2.5% fee” or similar so that you agree to cover the processing cost.
In the dedication or honor section, select “In honor of,” then type “John Smith” in the name field provided.

In the payment method area, select “Credit Card” and enter sample data such as “4111 1111 1111 1111” for the card number, “12/28” for expiration, “123” for CVV, and a sample billing ZIP code like “12345.”

Fill in the donor contact information fields (name, email, and address) with sample data, then click the “Donate,” “Submit Gift,” or equivalent button to complete the one-time $50 Missions Fund donation with the 2.5% fee covered and dedicated in honor of John Smith.

tasks.name: Join an online Wednesday small group for young adults that starts the soonest
tasks.description: Find online small groups aimed at young adults, filter to groups that meet on Wednesdays between 6:00 PM and 8:00 PM, and register for the one with the earliest upcoming start date.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Groups” or “Small Groups” link in the main navigation.
On the Groups page, open the filters panel and select the group type or audience filter labeled “Young Adults” or the closest equivalent.
In the meeting format filter, select “Online” as the format so that only online groups are shown.
In the day/time filters, select “Wednesday” as the meeting day and set the time range to between “6:00 PM” and “8:00 PM.”
Apply the filters, then review the resulting list of groups and identify the group whose listed start date is the earliest among the filtered options.
Click the “Details” or group name link for that earliest-starting Wednesday evening young adults online group, then click the “Join Group,” “Register,” or similar button on the group detail page.
Complete the registration form by entering sample contact information (such as name and email) and then click the “Submit,” “Join,” or equivalent button to join that specific small group.

tasks.name: Add the highest-rated recent forgiveness sermon under 35 minutes to your watch later list
tasks.description: Search the sermon library for sermons about forgiveness, filter to those from the last 2 years with at least a 4.5 star rating and a duration under 35 minutes, then add the highest-rated one to your Watch Later playlist.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Sermons” or “Messages” section in the main navigation.
Use the sermon search bar to type “forgiveness” and press Enter to view search results.
Open the filters panel and set the date filter to include only sermons from the “Last 2 Years.”
In the rating filter, select or adjust the slider so that only sermons with a rating of “4.5 stars or higher” are included.
In the duration or length filter, set the maximum duration to “35 minutes,” then apply the filters.

In the filtered results, look at the ratings displayed and identify the sermon with the highest rating; if more than one sermon has the same top rating, choose the one that appears first in the list and click its title or thumbnail to open the sermon detail page.

On the sermon detail page, click the “Add to Watch Later,” “Save to Playlist,” or equivalent button to add that forgiveness sermon under 35 minutes with at least a 4.5 star rating from the last 2 years to your Watch Later list.

tasks.name: Add 2 marriage books under $20 and 1 free devotional resource to your cart with free shipping
tasks.description: Use the resources or store section to add two physical books about marriage priced under $20 each and one free devotional resource to your cart, then select a free shipping option if available for orders over $30.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Resources,” “Store,” or “Bookstore” link in the main navigation.
In the store categories, select the category labeled “Marriage,” “Family & Marriage,” or the closest equivalent.
Use the price filter to set the maximum price to “$20” and apply the filter so that only marriage-related items costing $20 or less are shown.

Scroll through the filtered marriage resources and click on the first physical book (not an eBook or audio) in the list that costs less than or equal to $20, then click the “Add to Cart” button on its product page.
Return to the filtered marriage category page and select a second, different physical marriage book priced at $20 or less, then click “Add to Cart” for that second book.

Use the category or search controls to navigate to a section such as “Devotionals” or “Free Resources,” then locate an item that is clearly labeled as free (priced at $0) and click its product or resource card.
On the free devotional resource page, click an “Add to Cart,” “Add to Library,” or similar button to add that free devotional resource to your cart in addition to the two marriage books.

Click on the cart icon or “View Cart” link, then proceed to the shipping options step of checkout and select the shipping option labeled “Free Shipping” if it is available based on your order total; if multiple free options exist, choose the standard free shipping option.

entities.name: Devotional

fields.name: topic_tags
fields.description: Array of topic/tag strings such as 'anxiety', 'marriage'

entities.description: Daily devotional content items with metadata and tags for filtering.

entities.name: ReadingList

fields.description: Array of numbers representing child ages

entities.name: BibleReadingPlan

entities.name: ReadingPlanDay

entities.name: PrayerRequest

entities.description: Prayer requests submitted through the prayer form, including anonymous health-related requests.

entities.name: NewsletterSubscription

entities.name: DonationFund
entities.description: Donation funds, including Missions Fund, that users can give to.

entities.name: Donation

entities.name: SmallGroup

entities.name: GroupRegistration

entities.description: Registrations for specific small groups, including online Wednesday young adult groups.

entities.name: Sermon

entities.name: WatchLaterList

entities.name: ResourceCategory

entities.description: Product/resource categories such as Marriage and Free Devotional Resources (maps to categoryId URL parameters).

entities.name: Product

entities.name: Cart

entities.name: CartItem

entities.name: ShippingOption

entities.name: Order

entities.name: OrderItem
entities.description: Line items within an order, corresponding to products purchased from the store.
